title: Herding names the tail-stuck worktree; scout offers orphaned debt as fix-first knowledge work
read_first: skills/bee-herding/SKILL.md:142-160 and :309-314 (finished predicate D2 + anomaly branch)
action: Per scribing-integrity D4. Additive edits only, every existing sentence preserved. (1) bee-herding SKILL.md merge role (both the :142-148 restatement and the :309-314 D2 block, whichever is canonical — extend once, reference from the other): when a worktree fails D2 ONLY on phase — zero open/claimed cells, clean tree, HEAD on wt/<slug>, but phase pre-compounding-complete — the report line names it distinctly as tail-stuck (scribing/close owed) instead of the generic anomaly, and states the paved repair: the owner opens a session in that worktree to run the scribing/compounding tail (or waives with the logged flag); the role itself still takes no other action — report-only contract unchanged. (2) routing-and-contracts.md Session Scout: one short paragraph — when status reports orphaned scribing debt, surface it and offer it as fix-first knowledge work with the same one-line offer discipline as the capture-queue flush; never silently ignored. Regen: render_plugin -> onboard --apply -> release_manifest --write. Verify: greps for the new clauses + manifest check + test_misc doc coverage.

truths: tail-stuck state named distinctly in the herding merge role with the paved repair
scout offers orphaned debt with the capture-queue offer discipline
release manifest regenerated and check green

verify: rg -q 'tail-stuck' skills/bee-herding/SKILL.md && rg -q 'orphaned scribing debt' skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_misc.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Herding merge/dispatch roles name a tail-stuck worktree (D2 fails only on phase) distinctly from the generic anomaly, with the paved repair, report-only contract unchanged; scout offers orphaned scribing debt as fix-first knowledge work with the capture-queue offer discipline, naming state scribing-run as the repair verb.
